Rezaei on Monday dismissed the European Union's sanctions against Iranian individuals accused of threatening freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz.\nIn a statement, Rezaei said that Iran did not attach any value to the sanctions, adding that Tehran will continue its strategy of maintaining sovereignty over Hormuz.\n\nIsrael vows to press military campaign in Lebanon despite Iran's warnings\nIsraeli Defence Minister Israel Katz vowed Monday to press Israel's military campaign in Lebanon, despite a warning from Iran that any such action would prompt the Islamic republic to take \"severe\" measures.\n\"The IDF will continue to operate in Lebanon against the terrorist organisation Hezbollah,\" Katz said in a statement, adding that Israel would strike Beirut's Dahiyeh district in retaliation for every attack on north Israel.\n\nTanker crew evacuated after fire onboard vessel off Oman, says UKMTO\nA tanker experienced an onboard fire resulting in the evacuation of its crew, the United Kingdom Maritime Trade Operations (UKMTO) said on Monday, after receiving a report of an incident 15 nautical miles northeast of Oman's Masirah Island.\nUKMTO said no environmental impact had been reported so far.\n\nSirens sounded in northern Israel: military\nSirens sounded in northern Israel's Zar'it area on Monday after a projectile was identified as falling in an area where Israeli forces are operating in southern Lebanon, the Israeli military said, adding that no casualties were reported.\n\nUS airlines fuel costs soared in April to $6.5b amid Mideast war\nUS passenger airlines' fuel costs jumped 78% in April to nearly $6.5 billion over the same month last year as the conflict in the Middle East has sharply driven up fuel costs, the US Transportation Department said Monday.\nAirlines' fuel costs were up 26% over March and carriers used 2.6% less fuel in April over March, USDOT said in a monthly report.\nThe cost per gallon of fuel in April was $4.11, up $1.81 over April 2025.\n\nUS took no defence action to shield Tel Aviv in Iran-Israel exchange: CBS News\nThe United States military did not participate in recent Israeli attacks on Iran, which marked the first strikes since a ceasefire, CBS News reported on Monday.\nA senior White House reporter posted on X, citing a US official, that the Trump administration also did not order any defensive US action
